text:
There once was a wise old owl named Oliver. He lived high in the oak trees of Woodland Hollow. Each morning, he'd glide gracefully through the leaves, listening to the sweet songs of birds.

One day, Oliver heard whirling wings and saw a young bird learning to fly. Every time the young one spun around a branch, it let out a tiny shout, practicing its turning. Oliver watched with fond satisfaction.

After many turns, the young bird stopped. It peered up at Oliver with big, curious eyes.

"You're pretty good at circles, wouldn't you say?" Oliver remarked.

The young bird chirped excitedly. "Well, you're the first creature I've met I could spin like that!"

That day, Oliver decided to teach the bird something fun. He flew directly towards the young bird and spun around with him. The two danced through the air together for hours.

As the sun dipped below the trees, they perched peacefully, exhausted but merry. The young
